task1.title: Bar Chart: Internet Usage by Age Group
task1.prompt:
The chart below shows the percentage of internet users by age group in the UK between 2010 and 2020.

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.
task1.modelAnswer:
The visual data presents information about bar chart: internet usage by age group. Overall, the data reveals several notable trends and patterns that are worth examining in detail.

Looking at the information more closely, we can observe significant variations across the different categories presented. The most striking feature is the change that occurred over the period shown, with clear upward or downward trends evident in most cases.

In terms of specific figures, the highest values were recorded in certain categories, while others showed more moderate levels. Comparisons between different groups reveal interesting contrasts, with some showing parallel trends while others moved in opposite directions.

To summarise, the data illustrates important patterns and changes. The key findings highlight significant differences and trends that have important implications for understanding the subject matter.

task2.title: Opinion: Technology and Social Interaction
task2.prompt:
Some people believe that the increasing use of technology for communication is reducing the quality of human interaction.

Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

Write at least 250 words.
task2.modelAnswer:
The rapid advancement of communication technology has sparked considerable debate about its impact on human interaction. While some argue that it diminishes the quality of our connections, others contend that it strengthens them. This essay will examine both perspectives before presenting my own view.

On the one hand, critics of digital communication claim that it has led to a deterioration in the quality of human interaction. Face-to-face conversations, which involve nonverbal cues such as body language, eye contact, and tone of voice, are increasingly being replaced by text messages and social media posts. These forms of communication are often superficial and lack emotional depth. Furthermore, excessive screen time can lead to social isolation, as individuals spend more time engaging with their devices than with the people physically present around them.

On the other hand, proponents of technology argue that it has revolutionised communication by making it faster, more convenient, and more accessible. Video calling platforms allow families separated by thousands of kilometres to see and speak to each other regularly, maintaining bonds that would otherwise weaken. Social media enables people to reconnect with old friends and share important life events.

In my opinion, technology itself is neutral; its impact depends on how we use it. While excessive reliance on digital communication can indeed harm relationships, technology can also enhance human connection when used thoughtfully. The key is to strike a balance between the digital and physical worlds.

In conclusion, although technology presents certain risks to the quality of human interaction, it also offers unprecedented opportunities for connection.
